title: Integrate the committee-driven loop model across config, scripts, templates, and references
acceptance: The loop config defines research and committee requirements
The default committee defines product-manager, technical-architect, and user roles with 3 to 5 members each
The kit includes reference, script, and template support for the committee-driven loop model
notes: This establishes the first end-to-end committee-driven version before later iterations deepen durability and installer support.

title: Persist research and committee review conclusions into loop state
acceptance: The state model can store research findings, committee notes, and review decisions for the active iteration
The repo includes a script that records review findings into durable state
The stored review data remains concise and machine-readable
notes: Committee review should survive context loss instead of living only in reports or chat.

title: Read committee review state back into project data and reports
acceptance: Collected project data includes the latest recorded review state
Reports can render research findings and committee decisions from durable state with minimal manual flags
The data model stays consistent across state, project data, and report generation
notes: The loop should not require manual copy-paste of committee conclusions.

title: Add a project installation script for the committee-driven kit
acceptance: The repo contains a script that installs the loop kit into a target project root
The installer copies committee-driven references and scripts without requiring manual file-by-file copying
The installer avoids overwriting target-project-specific validation config unless explicitly requested
notes: This makes the upgraded committee loop easier to adopt in real projects.

title: Require recorded review state before reporting and publication
acceptance: The loop refuses to report or publish when required review-state artifacts are missing
Validation can detect missing durable review-state support
Protocol and README explain the new gate clearly
notes: This closes the gap between committee guidance and enforceable release gates.

title: Define lightweight councils, secretariat, and evaluator roster in config
acceptance: Config models product, architecture, and operator councils with persona ids and responsibilities
Config adds lightweight secretariat and evaluator definitions without breaking existing role rendering
The design remains concise enough for solo-project adoption
notes: Introduce clearer committee structure without making the kit enterprise-heavy.

title: Render council responsibilities and output contracts from config
acceptance: render-committee.py outputs council, secretariat, and evaluator responsibilities
Rendered output includes expected fields instead of only names
The output can be used directly as a review brief
notes: The current renderer is only a roster dump.

title: Add a backward-compatible review-state schema for councils and scope decisions
acceptance: State normalization supports research gate, council summaries, scope decision, evaluation, and escalation fields
Existing flat review-state usage remains readable during migration
The new schema stays concise and machine-friendly
notes: This creates room for stronger committee output without a transcript-heavy state file.

title: Teach capture-review.py to persist structured research-gate results
acceptance: capture-review.py can record research summary, evidence refs, quality score, and open gaps
Research gate output lands in the new review-state structure
The script still supports minimal usage for lightweight flows
notes: Research should be structured before scope decisions exist.

title: Capture council-specific feedback summaries instead of one flat committee list
acceptance: capture-review.py supports product, architecture, and operator council summaries
State distinguishes council feedback from final scope decisions
Dissent and evidence gaps can be attached without verbose transcripts
notes: Separate council outputs make disagreements auditable.

title: Persist selected goal, scope boundaries, assumptions, and required validation as a structured scope decision
acceptance: review_state stores selected_goal, scope_in, scope_out, assumptions, risks, required_validation, and stop_conditions
The schema supports dissent without forcing consensus
The structure is narrow enough to stay readable in JSON
notes: This turns committee output into an executable decision record.

title: Add a lightweight evaluator rubric reference and config hook
acceptance: The repo includes a rubric reference file for implementation readiness
Config points to the rubric and evaluator thresholds
The rubric remains small and repo-portable
notes: Evaluator scoring needs a stable contract before adding gates.

title: Allow capture-review.py to persist evaluator scores and pass-revise-fail outcome
acceptance: Evaluator results can be recorded in review_state with rubric version and weighted score
The stored output supports pass, revise, and fail outcomes
The design avoids mixing evaluator judgment with council summaries
notes: Evaluation should be explicit but lightweight.

title: Render structured scope decisions, dissent, and evaluation outcome in reports
acceptance: Reports include selected goal rationale, scope_in, scope_out, and required validation from durable state
Reports surface dissent and open gaps when present
Reports show evaluator outcome without dumping the entire state object
notes: The report should expose the stronger decision model to humans.

title: Expose structured review-state and evaluator readiness in project-data snapshots
acceptance: Project data includes the current structured review-state summary
Snapshot data indicates whether evaluation passed or is still incomplete
The summary avoids duplicating the full state tree
notes: Project data should reflect the stronger review process without becoming noisy.

title: Require a passing evaluator result before implementation begins when configured
acceptance: The loop can enforce evaluator pass before proceeding past pre-implementation gates
Missing or revise-fail evaluation blocks the iteration clearly
Validation covers the new gate
notes: Councils can advise, but evaluator pass should authorize implementation.

title: Model escalation policy for repeated review or validation failures
acceptance: Config defines lightweight escalation rules for repeated failures
review_state can record escalation reason and status
The policy stays deterministic and easy to explain
notes: Escalation should exist without introducing enterprise bureaucracy.

title: Surface stop conditions, open gaps, and escalation reasons in reports and docs
acceptance: Reports and protocol explain when the loop stops or escalates
Open gaps and stop conditions are rendered from durable state
The operator can understand why a version was blocked
notes: Human handoff quality depends on clear stop reasons.

title: Extend validate-kit to exercise the structured committee V2 flow
acceptance: validate-kit exercises council rendering, structured capture, evaluation capture, and gating behavior
Validation checks stay fast and deterministic
The kit can prove the lighter V2 architecture is wired end to end
notes: The upgraded committee architecture should be tested, not only documented.

title: Rewrite committee guidance around the lighter V2 architecture
acceptance: committee-driven-delivery.md documents councils, secretariat, evaluator, dissent, and escalation in lightweight terms
README and wrapper guidance stay aligned with the upgraded architecture
The docs explain the stronger model without sounding like enterprise governance
notes: Close the loop by documenting the final lighter-weight architecture.
